game_name: Need for Speed III: Hot Pursuit

danger_signals.visual_form: police car models in pursuit, distinctive police vehicle skins
danger_signals.meaning: active threat to player racer; indicates enforcement zone and chase initiation

danger_signals.visual_form: wall formation of police cars across road
danger_signals.meaning: imminent collision risk; forced stopping point ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: visual hazard markers on road surface (one side only)
danger_signals.meaning: tire damage zone; steering vulnerability if hit

danger_signals.visual_form: multiple police car models converging on player vehicle
audio + UI indicator of police communication
danger_signals.meaning: real-time threat awareness; police coordination acknowledgment

safety_signals.visual_form: clear road, no pursuing vehicles visible
safety_signals.meaning: safe racing zone; no enforcement active

safety_signals.visual_form: visual spaces between police car formation
clear visibility of race path, no obstacles
safety_signals.meaning: optimal racing conditions; low environmental hazard

safety_signals.visual_form: police focus shifts to AI racer, away from player
safety_signals.meaning: temporary reprieve; police targeting more dangerous competitor

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: exotic sports car models (Lamborghini Diablo SV, etc.)

faction_indicators.color_identity: police car standard livery (implied standard police coloring)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: police vehicle designation, official police car models
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: police sports car variants (selectable in PC version for role reversal)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: roadblocks, spike strips, patrol positioning on track

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: sports car variants distinct from player selection
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opposing racer vehicles with custom tuning visible through handling differences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: clean track environment, opponent racers visible, no police presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI competitors follow race line, focus on circuit completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: police vehicles present on track, roadblock formations deployed, spike strips visible on road surface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: police AI uses tactical blocking, ambush tactics, pursuit intensifies with multiple units; player must evade while racing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player operates police sports car variant, racer vehicles are targets, roadblock and spike strip tools deployed by player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player assumes enforcement role, controls blockade formation, deploys hazards, pursues racer targets across track

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: desert canyons, countryside villages, snow-capped mountain ranges visible; rain/racing tire selections affect visual grip indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: track conditions alter AI and player handling; tire selection becomes tactical choice

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: progressive police unit accumulation, coordinated blocking patterns, radio chatter frequency increase
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: police tactics become more aggressive; collision threat escalates; multiple vectors of attack

environmental_storytelling.example: desert canyons, countryside villages, snow-capped mountains communicate varied North American geography; each locale establishes different evasion challenges

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: audio cues + implicit UI indicators of coordination
environmental_storytelling.example: radio chatter reveals police locations, racer positions, roadblock deployment; reactions to collisions and speed violations establish enforcement narrative
roadblocks and spike strips positioned strategically on track communicate organized police response; gaps in formations suggest deliberate challenge design
single police car escalates to multiple pursuing units; visual accumulation of police vehicles establishes narrative of intensifying law enforcement commitment
hidden track routes provide visual evidence of experienced racer knowledge; implies underground racing culture awareness

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: blocks racer's intended path, deploys roadblocks ahead, forms ambush formations with multiple units
shifts focus away from player when rival driving is deemed more dangerous; resets player pursuit priority
coordinates spike strip placement (one side of road only), creates wall formations with exploitable gaps
forced stop near police vehicle; pursuit mode termination
last-place finisher removed from race; visual elimination from circuit
carries tuning and handling characteristics across rounds; demonstrates learning or consistent threat level

notes: Need for Speed III: Hot Pursuit establishes a two-faction dynamic (racers vs. law enforcement) with clear visual and behavioral distinction. The Pursuit system is the primary manifestation layer—police presence, tactical obstacles (roadblocks/spike strips), and radio chatter create a layered threat communication system. World-state shifts between standard racing and hot pursuit mode alter track visual complexity and AI behavioral priorities. The role-reversal mechanic (player as police) inverts faction perception but maintains the same visual manifestation logic. Environmental settings (desert, rural, mountains) serve as static storytelling backdrop rather than dynamic world-state indicators. Car tuning system affects handling visibility but not faction identity—all cars remain faction-neutral until assigned role.
